# vtimestamp

> Decentralized document timestamping service built on the Verus blockchain.
> Users hash documents locally (SHA-256), store the hash on their VerusID
> (a self-sovereign blockchain identity), and anyone can verify the timestamp
> without an account. Documents never leave the user's device.

vtimestamp creates cryptographic proof that a document existed at a specific
point in time. The proof is permanently stored on the Verus blockchain, tied
to the user's VerusID. Unlike centralized timestamping services, the proof
survives even if vtimestamp itself shuts down.

## Core Pages
- [How It Works](https://vtimestamp.com/how-it-works): Technical explanation of the timestamping and verification flow
- [Compare](https://vtimestamp.com/compare): Detailed comparison vs other blockchain and traditional timestamping methods
- [Use Cases](https://vtimestamp.com/use-cases): Applications for IP, legal, research, creative work, and compliance
- [FAQ](https://vtimestamp.com/faq): Common questions about privacy, cost, verification, and trust model
- [Developer Docs](https://vtimestamp.com/developers): VDXF key reference, on-chain data structure, CLI verification, MCP server integration

## Tools
- [Verify a Timestamp](https://vtimestamp.com/verify): Public verification tool (no login required)
- [Create a Timestamp](https://vtimestamp.com/create): Create a new timestamp (requires VerusID login)

## Optional
- [About](https://vtimestamp.com/about): Mission, background, and open-source info
- [GitHub](https://github.com/vdappdev2/vtimestamp): Source code (MIT license)
- [MCP Server (read)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vtimestamp-mcp): AI agent integration for verifying timestamps
- [MCP Server (write)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vtimestamp-mcp-write): AI agent integration for creating timestamps
- [Full Content](https://vtimestamp.com/llms-full.txt): Complete site content in a single document
